title: Data reference (object reference to a Reference Data Layer)
description: The canonical in-field reference shape (UDLM ADR-012) — a field points at shared, governed data instead of inlining a copy. Modelled on the Kubernetes ObjectReference / ownerReference idiom: ref_uuid is AUTHORITATIVE (resolution uses only the uuid), ref_name is ADVISORY (human-readable, kept honest but never the resolution key). A data reference MUST resolve to an ACTIVE `layer` record with layer_type: reference_data whose reference_data_type equals the reference's declared reference_data_type (foundations/layering-and-versioning.md §3.7). Referential integrity is enforced by registry/tools/validate.py (check_data_references): a dangling ref_uuid, a type mismatch, or a wrong advisory ref_name is a hard failure — invalid edges resolve deterministically, never silently (minimal-custom-surface-and-graph-resilience findings #1/#2). Other schemas that allow a field to be a reference $ref this $defs/data_reference in a oneOf against the inline value.

ref_uuid.description: AUTHORITATIVE. RFC 9562 v4 uuid of the reference_data layer. Because a layer version is IMMUTABLE (any change mints a new uuid+version, ADR-012), this pins an exact, reproducible version. Resolution uses only this. A uuid that resolves to nothing is a dangling reference (hard fail), never a silent rebind onto a same-named record.

ref_name.description: ADVISORY. The resolved layer's handle or name, carried for human readability. Validated to match the resolved target at authoring time (honesty gate) but never used to resolve.

ref_version.description: ADVISORY. The semver of the pinned version, recorded for readability + drift detection. Validated to equal the resolved layer's version (honesty gate). Impact analysis reads it to report when a newer version of the same handle exists (the reference is pinned to a superseded version).

reference_data_type.description: The kind the reference expects (e.g. network_zone, os_image, location, topology_capability). Must equal the resolved layer's reference_data_type — the match axis that stops a field from binding data of the wrong kind.
